seo.title: Building AI Coding Assistant with Pydantic AI + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai coding assistant with Pydantic AI + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building an AI Coding Assistant with Pydantic AI enables type-safe, structured code generation with repository context and iterative debugging. Guardrails are essential to catch subtle failures like validator rejections on valid-but-unexpected outputs, masked tool errors, and streaming validation breakdowns. Vex integration ensures production reliability by validating code correctness, security, and compliance before deployment.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Coding Assistant Architecture with Pydantic AI
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture starts with a code-aware LLM agent that ingests repository context via Pydantic AI's structured tools. It generates code proposals, executes them in a sandbox, runs tests, and iterates based on validation feedback. Pydantic models enforce output structure while Vex result validators check for security and correctness.

Key components include: repository loader for context injection, sandbox executor for safe code runs, test validator for functionality, and debug loop for refinement. Nested tool calls handle dependency injection across execution phases.

Streaming responses deliver partial code with real-time validation, preventing mid-generation failures.

risks.name: Result validator rejecting valid but unexpected model outputs
risks.description: Pydantic models reject semantically correct code that deviates from strict schemas
risks.mitigation: Vex semantic validation layer before Pydantic enforcement

risks.name: Tool retry logic masking persistent underlying errors
risks.description: Sandbox failures or test timeouts get retried indefinitely without root cause detection
risks.mitigation: Vex failure pattern detection and escalation

risks.name: Dependency injection context not available in nested tool calls
risks.description: Repo context lost during iterative debugging tool chains

risks.name: Streaming structured responses failing mid-generation validation
risks.description: Partial code streams break Pydantic validation, causing incomplete generations

implementation.title: Define Core Agent with Pydantic AI
implementation.description: Create the main coding agent with structured code output model and repository context tool
implementation.code:
from pydantic_ai import Agent, tool
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

class CodeProposal(BaseModel):
    code: str = Field(..., description="Generated Python code")
    explanation: str = Field(..., description="Why this solution works")
    tests: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)

@tool
async def load_repo_context(repo_path: str) -> str:
    """Load relevant repository context for coding tasks"""
    return f"Context from {repo_path}: ..."

coding_agent = Agent(
    "CodeGen",
    model="gpt-4o",
    result_model=CodeProposal,
    tools=[load_repo_context]
)

implementation.title: Sandbox Executor Tool
implementation.description: Safe execution environment with test validation for generated code
implementation.code:
import subprocess
import tempfile
from pydantic_ai import tool

@tool
async def execute_code(code: str, test_code: list[str] = None) -> dict:
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        code_path = f'{tmpdir}/generated.py'
        with open(code_path, 'w') as f:
            f.write(code)
        
        result = subprocess.run(
            ['python', code_path],
            capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=30
        )
        
        return {
            'exit_code': result.returncode,
            'stdout': result.stdout,
            'stderr': result.stderr,
            'passed': result.returncode == 0
        }

vexIntegration.title: Add Vex Result Validator to Agent
vexIntegration.description: Integrate Vex as result_validator to catch validator rejections and semantic errors
vexIntegration.code:
import vex

from pydantic_ai import Agent

# Initialize Vex client
vex_client = vex.Client(api_key="your-vex-api-key")

@vex.result_validator(
    checks=[
        "code_correctness",
        "security_vulnerability_scanning",
        "no_credential_leakage",
        "dependency_safety"
    ],
    risk_profile="medium"
)
def validate_code_proposal(proposal: CodeProposal) -> bool:
    """Vex-powered validation before Pydantic acceptance"""
    result = vex_client.validate(
        content=proposal.code,
        language="python",
        context={
            "task_type": "code_generation",
            "repo_context": True
        }
    )
    return result.passed

# Attach to agent
coding_agent.result_validator = validate_code_proposal

vexIntegration.title: Vex Tool Call Monitoring
vexIntegration.description: Monitor nested tool calls for dependency injection failures and retry masking
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import span

from pydantic_ai import Agent

async def monitored_debug_loop(task: str, repo_path: str):
    with span("debug_loop") as debug_span:
        debug_span.set_tag("task", task)
        debug_span.set_tag("repo", repo_path)
        
        context = await load_repo_context(repo_path)
        
        for i in range(5):
            with span("agent_call"):
                proposal = await coding_agent.run(task + context)
                
            with span("execution"):
                exec_result = await execute_code(proposal.code)
                
            if not exec_result['passed']:
                debug_span.add_event("execution_failed", exec_result['stderr'])
                vex_client.report_failure(
                    error=exec_result['stderr'],
                    context=proposal.code
                )
            else:
                return proposal

vexIntegration.title: Streaming Validation with Vex Checkpoints
vexIntegration.description: Validate streaming code generation at checkpoints to prevent mid-stream failures
vexIntegration.code:
import asyncio
from vex.streaming import StreamingValidator

async def stream_validated_code(task: str):
    stream_validator = StreamingValidator(
        vex_client,
        checkpoints=[100, 500, 1000],  # chars
        checks=["partial_code_syntax", "security_checkpoint"]
    )
    
    async for chunk in coding_agent.stream(task):
        validated_chunk = await stream_validator.validate(chunk)
        if not validated_chunk.valid:
            await stream_validator.recover(chunk)
            continue
        
        yield validated_chunk.content
    
    # Final validation
    final_result = await stream_validator.finalize()
    if not final_result.passed:
        raise ValueError(f"Final validation failed: {final_result.errors}")
